title: lomatium laevigatum
intro: Lomatium laevigatum, also known as Western lomatium or Western biscuitroot, is a perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing symptoms of the common cold and flu, as well as providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Lomatium laevigatum can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
